page: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A defining feature of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, offering 55,920 GB of local HDD storage. This makes it highly suitable for storage-intensive applications. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, facilitating efficient data transfer for large-scale storage operations. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the middle 50% tier. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and gzip compression benchmarks. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput are average. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores are weak, and LLM inference for medium 7B models is poor, highlighting that this instance is not designed for compute-heavy or AI inference workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d3en.2xlarge offers a high density of local HDD storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a cost-effective choice for massive data storage without paying for excess CPU power. It is best suited for workloads such as data warehousing, large-scale file systems, MapReduce, log processing, and big data applications that require high-capacity local storage and moderate compute capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for network and storage-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor, it features 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a massive 55,920 GB of local HDD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, though single-core and multi-core computational scaling benchmarks are relatively weak. Lacking GPU acceleration, it is not suited for machine learning or high-performance computing. Instead, its high storage density makes it highly cost-efficient for data warehousing, big data analytics, MapReduce, and large-scale file systems where massive local capacity is required alongside moderate processing power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 55,920 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for big data and file system workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 55,920 GB of local HDD storage.
tagline: A dense storage instance offering massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for big data, file systems, and data warehousing.
bullet_points: Features 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Provides an exceptional 55,920 GB of local HDD storage capacity
Delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for data warehousing, MapReduce, and large-scale file systems
